title: CompetitiveIntelExtraction_v2
description: Per-speaker competitive intelligence extraction from AACR 2026 talks. Identifies clinical failures, trial design flaws, mechanistic blindspots, and strategic opportunities. CRITICAL RULES: (1) All fields must be grounded in transcript evidence — no speculation. (2) All array fields must be present but MAY be [] if no evidence exists. (3) Do NOT invent vulnerabilities or opportunities not supported by the transcript. (4) rhetorical_signals must contain direct quotes or close paraphrases, never labels.

talk_id.description: Must match the talk_id from Schema A for the same talk.

speaker_name.description: Full name of the speaker — must match Schema A speaker.name for the same talk.

institution_or_pharma.description: Institution or company — must match Schema A speaker.affiliation.

session_title.description: Title of the parent session — must match Schema A session_title.

talk_title.description: Title of this specific talk — must match Schema A talk_title.

rhetorical_signals.description: Direct quoted phrases or close paraphrases from the transcript that reveal hedging, overconfidence, narrative pivoting, or data omission. Each entry must be a specific quote or paraphrase — NOT a label. e.g. 'Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped' or 'Skipped OS data entirely, presented only PFS'. Leave empty array if no signals detected.

presentation_type.description: Type of presentation: clinical_trial_readout = Phase 1-3 data; translational_science = bench-to-bedside; basic_science = mechanistic/preclinical; platform_showcase = technology demo; award_lecture = career retrospective; industry_pitch = company presenting pipeline asset; panel_discussion = no primary data.

vulnerability_identified.description: Explicit or implicit admissions of failure, resistance, or clinical dead-ends. Only populate if there is actual transcript evidence.

failing_compound_or_target.description: The specific drug, target, or approach that is failing or has limitations.

mechanistic_blindspot.description: Biological pathway, paralog redundancy, or feedback loop the speaker is ignoring or has not addressed. Must be mechanistically specific — not generic.

unexplained_resistance_quote.description: Direct quote or close paraphrase where the speaker admits tumor escape without explanation. Leave null if not present.

ghost_responder_admission.description: Mention of 'exceptional responders', 'subset efficacy', or 'some patients responded' in an otherwise failed/mediocre cohort. Quote or paraphrase. Leave null if not present.

hidden_tox_signal.description: Minimization of dose-limiting toxicities, narrow therapeutic windows, or off-target effects. Quote or paraphrase. Leave null if not present.

evidence_strength.description: How strongly the transcript supports this vulnerability.

trial_dilution_risk.description: Trial design flaws that risk diluting signal or guaranteeing failure. Only populate if the speaker described a trial design with identifiable flaws.

trial_name_or_nct.description: Trial name or NCT number if mentioned.

flawed_enrollment_criteria.description: Specific flaw: e.g. 'Enrolling all-comers by histology rather than molecular subtype', 'No ctDNA pre-screening', 'Mixed MMRd and MMRp patients in same arm'.

missing_biomarker.description: The specific molecular biomarker that should stratify patients but is absent from the design.

static_vs_dynamic_failure.description: Failure to track dynamic resistance (e.g. no on-treatment ctDNA, no serial biopsies). Leave null if not applicable.

statistical_concern.description: Underpowering, inappropriate endpoint, or other statistical design flaw. Leave null if not applicable.

competitive_moat_weakness.description: Structural vulnerabilities in the competitor's platform, chemistry, or clinical strategy revealed in the talk.

vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry.description: What specific chemistry, platform, or IP is vulnerable. e.g. 'Covalent KRAS G12C inhibitor — only works on G12C, not G12D or G12V'.

ip_vulnerability_note.description: What the speaker revealed about their chemistry or platform that suggests circumventability. Based only on what was said — no patent analysis. e.g. 'Speaker disclosed the linker chemistry in detail, suggesting the core scaffold is the only novel element'.

scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck.description: Manufacturing, COGS, or scalability limitations mentioned. Leave null if not mentioned.

clinical_strategy_weakness.description: Weakness in their clinical development strategy. e.g. 'Pursuing broad label rather than biomarker-selected population — will dilute signal'.

cognitive_dissonance.description: Specific contradictions between the speaker's high-resolution science and their low-resolution clinical conclusions. Each entry must cite both the scientific observation and the contradictory conclusion. e.g. 'Speaker showed CD8 T cell exhaustion in 80% of tumors by scRNA-seq, then concluded that checkpoint blockade alone should be sufficient — ignoring the exhaustion data'.

data_maturity_assessment.description: Assessment of how mature and reliable the evidence base is.

sample_size_adequacy.description: Whether the sample sizes support the conclusions drawn.

follow_up_adequacy.description: Whether follow-up duration is sufficient for the endpoints claimed.

key_data_gaps.description: Specific data that was conspicuously absent. e.g. 'No OS data presented', 'No biomarker-stratified analysis', 'No comparison to standard of care'.

crispro_opportunity.description: Strategic opportunities for CrisPRO platform based on what was presented. Grounded in transcript evidence; flag what external data would be needed to confirm.

description.description: Specific description of the opportunity grounded in what the speaker said.

transcript_evidence.description: Direct quote or close paraphrase from the transcript that supports this opportunity.

crispro_angle.description: How CrisPRO's computational spine or biomarker platform specifically addresses this gap.

external_validation_needed.description: External data sources needed to confirm this opportunity. e.g. ['ClinicalTrials.gov: check enrollment criteria for NCT04165772', 'PubMed: resistance mechanisms for KRAS G12C inhibitors', 'SEC filings: pipeline status for Amgen sotorasib'].

priority.description: Estimated strategic priority based on transcript evidence alone.

cited_competitors.description: Named competing programs, companies, or drugs mentioned in the talk.

context.description: How they were mentioned — favorably, critically, or neutrally.

sentiment.description: How the competitor was framed: favorable = speaker praised them, critical = speaker highlighted their failures/limitations, neutral = factual mention, comparative = used as benchmark.

unresolved_questions.description: Explicit scientific or clinical questions the speaker raised but did not answer. High-value for identifying research gaps.

nct_numbers_to_check.description: NCT numbers mentioned — check ClinicalTrials.gov for enrollment criteria, status, and results.

assets_to_track.description: Drug names or programs to track in pipeline databases (Citeline, Evaluate, GlobalData).

companies_to_monitor.description: Companies whose pipeline status should be checked.
